<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The issue is clear. The `keyExtractor` on line 77 uses `String(item.id)`, and the TMDB API can return the same movie on multiple pages. When `appendNextPage` appends results on line 87 of `usePaginatedMovies.ts`, it does `setMovies(prev => [...prev, ...res.results])` without deduplicating.

The fix should be in `usePaginatedMovies.ts` — deduplicate movies when appending new pages. This is the cleanest solution because it fixes the issue at the data layer rather than at the UI layer.</details></think>

_**Agent (claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking)**_

Found the issue. The TMDB API can return the same movie across different pages. When `appendNextPage` runs (line 87 in `usePaginatedMovies.ts`), it concatenates new results without deduplicating:

```87:87:src/hooks/usePaginatedMovies.ts
      setMovies(prev => [...prev, ...res.results]);
```

The `MovieRow` FlatList uses `item.id` as the key (line 77), so duplicate movie IDs cause the React key collision warning.

The fix is to deduplicate at the data layer when appending pages:

_**Agent (claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking)**_

_**Agent (claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking)**_

No lint issues. Here's a summary of the fix:

**What changed:** `src/hooks/usePaginatedMovies.ts` — the `appendNextPage` function now deduplicates movies before appending them to the list.

**Why:** The TMDB API can return the same movie on different pages. When paginating, the old code blindly concatenated new results onto the existing array, creating duplicate entries with the same `id`. Since `MovieRow`'s FlatList uses `item.id` as the `keyExtractor`, React saw two children with the same key (e.g. `1084187`).

**How:** Before merging new results, we build a `Set` of existing movie IDs and filter out any duplicates from the incoming page. This ensures every movie in the list has a unique ID.
